100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 6, 1926, the United Kingdom faced growing tensions as labor unrest surged, with a nationwide general strike looming over disputes in the coal industry. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate began deliberations on the McNary-Haugen Bill, aimed at stabilizing agricultural prices amidst economic uncertainty. Internationally, political turmoil continued in Germany, where the government struggled to maintain order following the rise of extremist factions.
Updated: Saturday, June 6, 2026 at 07:59:13 AM CDT
